2603.20673.title: PAVE: Premise-Aware Validation and Editing for Retrieval-Augmented LLMs
2603.20673.paper_summary: Retrieval-augmented language models can retrieve relevant evidence yet still commit to answers before explicitly checking whether the retrieved context supports the conclusion. We present PAVE (Premise-Grounded Answer Validation and Editing), an inference-time validation layer for evidence-grounded question answering. PAVE decomposes retrieved context into question-conditioned atomic facts, drafts an answer, scores how well that draft is supported by the extracted premises, and revises low-support outputs before finalization. The resulting trace makes answer commitment auditable at the level of explicit premises, support scores, and revision decisions. In controlled ablations with a fixed retriever and backbone, PAVE outperforms simpler post-retrieval baselines in two evidence-grounded QA settings, with the largest gain reaching 32.7 accuracy points on a span-grounded benchmark. We view these findings as proof-of-concept evidence that explicit premise extraction plus support-gated revision can strengthen evidence-grounded consistency in retrieval-augmented LLM systems.
2603.20673.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強的語言模型可以檢索相關證據，但在明確檢查檢索到的上下文是否支持結論之前，仍然會做出回答。我們提出了 PAVE（前提基礎的答案驗證與編輯），這是一個針對基於證據的問題回答的推理時驗證層。PAVE 將檢索到的上下文分解為問題條件的原子事實，草擬答案，評分該草擬答案受到提取前提支持的程度，並在最終確定之前修正低支持的輸出。最終的追蹤使答案承諾在明確的前提、支持分數和修訂決策的層面上可審計。在固定檢索器和骨幹的控制性消融實驗中，PAVE 在兩個基於證據的 QA 設置中超越了更簡單的後檢索基準，最大的增益在一個基於跨度的基準上達到 32.7 的準確度點。我們將這些發現視為概念證明的證據，表明明確的前提提取加上支持門控修訂可以加強檢索增強 LLM 系統中的基於證據的一致性。
2603.20673.author: Tianyi Huang et.al.
2603.20673.authors: Tianyi Huang, Caden Yang, Emily Yin, Eric Wang, Michael Zhang
